findings.problem: The stop-completion path can publish an internally inconsistent ControlDebugSnapshot. In the STOPPING branch, wheel_targets are computed from the first motion decision before stop completion is re-evaluated, but the snapshot later records motion_phase and effective_speed_target from final_motion. On the cycle where the second evaluation advances STOPPING to DISARMED, the snapshot can report phase=DISARMED and effective_speed_target=0 while left/right wheel targets still reflect the earlier STOPPING state.
findings.evidence: new/code/runtime/control_loop.cpp:511-512 computes wheel_targets from motion.effective_speed_target before stop completion is re-evaluated; new/code/runtime/control_loop.cpp:536-546 may replace motion with final_motion after the zero-command stop-completion check; new/code/runtime/control_loop.cpp:566-571 stores motion_phase/effective_speed_target from final_motion but left_speed_target/right_speed_target from the earlier wheel_targets. This conflicts with the checkpoint contract that exported wheel-level evidence stays aligned with the lifecycle-owned effective target in the same decision surface (openspec/changes/decouple-assistant-telemetry-and-dual-wheel-pid/specs/dual-wheel-motion-control/spec.md:35-49, openspec/changes/decouple-assistant-telemetry-and-dual-wheel-pid/design.md:171-179, openspec/changes/decouple-assistant-telemetry-and-dual-wheel-pid/design.md:254-267).
findings.recommendation: Recompute or clear wheel_targets after the second STOPPING evaluation so all snapshot fields come from the same motion decision. A safe fix is to derive snapshot targets from final_motion, or explicitly zero them whenever final_motion leaves the drive-allowed STOPPING state before publishing control_debug_snapshot/control.snapshot.
